title: [Bug]: NATS monitoring JSON parser accepts duplicate keys
summary: The NATS server monitoring collector rejects non-standard JSON constants but still accepts duplicate object keys from monitoring endpoint responses.
observed: A monitoring response with duplicate server_id fields can be parsed with only the last value retained.
expected: Monitoring endpoint JSON should reject duplicate keys as ambiguous before field allow-list extraction occurs.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that passes duplicate-key monitoring JSON into the endpoint parser.
impact: Observability data can drive operational decisions. Ambiguous monitoring JSON should not be converted into public snapshots or Prometheus output.
delivery_semantics: No JetStream ACK behavior changes. This fix affects the optional observability plane only.
security: The fix must not expose NATS endpoint URLs, private host details, credentials, or full monitoring payloads in errors.
acceptance: A focused regression test proves duplicate monitoring JSON keys are rejected.
Non-standard constant rejection remains in place.
Valid monitoring endpoint JSON continues to parse.
tests: Run the focused regression test, NATS monitoring tests, observability CLI tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update CHANGELOG.md and observability documentation to mention strict monitoring JSON parsing.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published. Include the release tag, regression evidence, verification evidence, and any remaining limitations.
